gaps.title: State serialization round-trip fidelity failure
gaps.breaks: AI conversation state gets corrupted during serialization/deserialization, leading to hallucinated data or state loss
gaps.scenario: A conversation with 15 turns, containing complex nested variables and a 500-token technique stack is serialized to Redis, then deserialized. The restored state has missing history entries, incorrect token_count, and a modified current_node, causing the conversation to take a wrong turn
gaps.prompt: Write a test for state_serialization.py that creates a complex conversation state with nested variables, long history, and a technique stack. Serialize it to Redis, then deserialize it and verify all fields match exactly. Include edge cases with special characters, unicode, and maximum length values.

gaps.title: Redis/PostgreSQL failover data loss
gaps.breaks: When Redis fails, state fallback to PostgreSQL doesn't preserve all state components, causing silent data truncation
gaps.scenario: A conversation state is saved with Redis as primary, then Redis crashes. When the system attempts to load the state, it only retrieves the current_node and variables from PostgreSQL, losing the history and technique_stack. The conversation appears to reset to an earlier state
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates Redis failure by mocking Redis operations to raise exceptions. Verify that when Redis is unavailable, the system correctly falls back to PostgreSQL and retrieves the complete state without data loss. Test both save and load operations during Redis failure.

gaps.title: Distributed lock contention causing deadlock
gaps.breaks: Multiple workers trying to modify the same conversation state simultaneously acquire locks in different order, causing permanent deadlock
gaps.scenario: Two support agents are working on the same conversation simultaneously. One acquires the lock for state modification, the other tries to acquire the lock for checkpoint creation. They wait indefinitely for each other, causing the conversation to become unresponsive
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent access to the same state by multiple workers. Create a scenario where two workers attempt to modify the same state simultaneously with different operations (e.g., one updating state, another creating a checkpoint). Verify the system handles this gracefully without deadlocks.

gaps.title: Tenant isolation breach in state keys
gaps.breaks: State keys are not properly scoped by tenant, causing data leakage between customers
gaps.scenario: Company A and Company B both have conversations with the same conversation_id. Due to missing company_id in state keys, when Company B's agent loads a conversation, they accidentally see Company A's conversation state
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates identical conversation states for two different tenants (company_id). Verify that when loading state by conversation_id, the system returns only the state for the correct tenant. Test edge cases where company_id is missing or malformed.

gaps.title: GSD transition validation bypass
gaps.breaks: Invalid state transitions are allowed when they shouldn't be, violating the state machine rules
gaps.scenario: A conversation is in DIAGNOSIS state, but the system allows a direct transition to FOLLOW_UP, skipping the required RESOLUTION state. This breaks the conversation flow and customer experience
gaps.prompt: Write a comprehensive test for gsd_engine.py that validates all possible state transitions. Create test cases for each variant (mini_parwa, parwa, parwa_high) and verify that only allowed transitions are permitted. Include tests for invalid transitions, missing transition reasons, and variant-specific restrictions.

gaps.title: Auto-escalation trigger condition race condition
gaps.breaks: Frustration score calculation and escalation decision happen at slightly different times, causing missed escalations
gaps.scenario: A customer's frustration score crosses the 80 threshold between the time it's calculated and when the auto-escalation check runs. The system fails to escalate, requiring manual intervention
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates the timing race between frustration score calculation and escalation decision. Create a scenario where frustration crosses the threshold during this window and verify the system still correctly triggers escalation. Test with different timing variations.

gaps.title: History ring buffer overflow corruption
gaps.breaks: When conversation history exceeds the buffer limit, the system doesn't properly remove oldest entries, causing memory issues
gaps.scenario: A conversation reaches the maximum history length (e.g., 100 entries). When a new turn is added, the oldest entry is not properly removed, causing the history to grow beyond limits and potentially crash the system
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates a conversation history exceeding the maximum buffer size. Verify that when new entries are added, the oldest entries are properly removed and the history size remains constant. Test edge cases with exactly at the limit and just over the limit.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 7

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: State serialization round-trip fidelity failure
What breaks: AI conversation state gets corrupted during serialization/deserialization, leading to hallucinated data or state loss
Real scenario: A conversation with 15 turns, containing complex nested variables and a 500-token technique stack is serialized to Redis, then deserialized. The restored state has missing history entries, incorrect token_count, and a modified current_node, causing the conversation to take a wrong turn
AI agent prompt: Write a test for state_serialization.py that creates a complex conversation state with nested variables, long history, and a technique stack. Serialize it to Redis, then deserialize it and verify all fields match exactly. Include edge cases with special characters, unicode, and maximum length values.

GAP 2
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Redis/PostgreSQL failover data loss
What breaks: When Redis fails, state fallback to PostgreSQL doesn't preserve all state components, causing silent data truncation
Real scenario: A conversation state is saved with Redis as primary, then Redis crashes. When the system attempts to load the state, it only retrieves the current_node and variables from PostgreSQL, losing the history and technique_stack. The conversation appears to reset to an earlier state
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates Redis failure by mocking Redis operations to raise exceptions. Verify that when Redis is unavailable, the system correctly falls back to PostgreSQL and retrieves the complete state without data loss. Test both save and load operations during Redis failure.

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Distributed lock contention causing deadlock
What breaks: Multiple workers trying to modify the same conversation state simultaneously acquire locks in different order, causing permanent deadlock
Real scenario: Two support agents are working on the same conversation simultaneously. One acquires the lock for state modification, the other tries to acquire the lock for checkpoint creation. They wait indefinitely for each other, causing the conversation to become unresponsive
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent access to the same state by multiple workers. Create a scenario where two workers attempt to modify the same state simultaneously with different operations (e.g., one updating state, another creating a checkpoint). Verify the system handles this gracefully without deadlocks.

GAP 4
Severity: HIGH
Title: Tenant isolation breach in state keys
What breaks: State keys are not properly scoped by tenant, causing data leakage between customers
Real scenario: Company A and Company B both have conversations with the same conversation_id. Due to missing company_id in state keys, when Company B's agent loads a conversation, they accidentally see Company A's conversation state
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates identical conversation states for two different tenants (company_id). Verify that when loading state by conversation_id, the system returns only the state for the correct tenant. Test edge cases where company_id is missing or malformed.

GAP 5
Severity: HIGH
Title: GSD transition validation bypass
What breaks: Invalid state transitions are allowed when they shouldn't be, violating the state machine rules
Real scenario: A conversation is in DIAGNOSIS state, but the system allows a direct transition to FOLLOW_UP, skipping the required RESOLUTION state. This breaks the conversation flow and customer experience
AI agent prompt: Write a comprehensive test for gsd_engine.py that validates all possible state transitions. Create test cases for each variant (mini_parwa, parwa, parwa_high) and verify that only allowed transitions are permitted. Include tests for invalid transitions, missing transition reasons, and variant-specific restrictions.

GAP 6
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Auto-escalation trigger condition race condition
What breaks: Frustration score calculation and escalation decision happen at slightly different times, causing missed escalations
Real scenario: A customer's frustration score crosses the 80 threshold between the time it's calculated and when the auto-escalation check runs. The system fails to escalate, requiring manual intervention
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates the timing race between frustration score calculation and escalation decision. Create a scenario where frustration crosses the threshold during this window and verify the system still correctly triggers escalation. Test with different timing variations.

GAP 7
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: History ring buffer overflow corruption
What breaks: When conversation history exceeds the buffer limit, the system doesn't properly remove oldest entries, causing memory issues
Real scenario: A conversation reaches the maximum history length (e.g., 100 entries). When a new turn is added, the oldest entry is not properly removed, causing the history to grow beyond limits and potentially crash the system
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates a conversation history exceeding the maximum buffer size. Verify that when new entries are added, the oldest entries are properly removed and the history size remains constant. Test edge cases with exactly at the limit and just over the limit.
